gwhappen:
Recently, you may have noticed that global warming has been getting some attention in the news. Global warming refers to the idea that the world’s average temperature has been increasing over the past 150 years, may be increasing more in the future, and that the world’s climate may change as a result.  



What do you think: Do you think that global warming is happening?
second order Q: To the best of your knowledge, what percentage of Americans would AGREE with the statement that "global warming is happening"? Type a number from 0 (no one) to 100 (everyone).
gwhuman: Assuming global warming is happening, do you think it is...
neighbors:
About
  how often do you hear other people you know (your family, friends,
  co-workers, etc.) talk about global warming?
exp_emphasis: Is your current home located in the right place on this map?
slharmpersonal: How much do you think sea-level rise will harm you personally in the next 10 years?
slharmtime: How soon do you think sea-level rise will harm you personally?
slharmbayarea: How much do you think sea-level rise will harm people in your local community?
slharmUS: How much do you think sea-level rise will harm people in the United States?
slharmdeveloping: How much do you think sea-level rise will harm people in developing countries?
slharmfuture: How much do you think sea-level rise will harm future generations?
linkGQ: Do you think the risk of sea-level rise is linked to global warming?
slneighbor:
About
  how often do you hear other people you know (your family, friends,

SysRiskElsUncertain: Thinking about the map you just saw, do you think it displayed a realistic scenario for flooding in your neighborhood?
SysRiskElse: Thinking about the map you just saw, how much do you think that the flooding (if any) that it showed would impact your ability to get around (e.g. commuting to work, going shopping, running errands, etc.)?

Q737:
When was the last time your community was impacted by a flood, regardless of whether it
was associated with a federally declared disaster?
Q739_1: Are the following statements true or false for your community? - Your community is vulnerable to floods.
Q739_2: Are the following statements true or false for your community? - Your community’s flood risk can change over time due to new weather patterns, development, and other factors.
Q739_3: Are the following statements true or false for your community? - Your community depends on the local government for flood risk information they can use to protect themselves and their property from flooding.
Q739_4: Are the following statements true or false for your community? - There are actions that your community can take that would help the community recover more quickly after a flood.
Q739_5: Are the following statements true or false for your community? - There are many available resources, both technical and financial, that can be used to reduce your community’s flood risk.
Q740_1: Do you agree or disagree with the following policies? - Limiting how development occurs in flood prone areas.
Q740_2: Do you agree or disagree with the following policies? - Increasing the number of residents and business owners that invest in flood insurance, regardless of the property’s location relative to a floodplain.
Q740_3: Do you agree or disagree with the following policies? - Removing existing structures from flood prone areas.
Q740_4: Do you agree or disagree with the following policies? - Constructing small flood control structures intended to prevent flood damage.
Q741: When you want to know about flood risks in your community, how do you get information? (Check all that apply)
insurance: Which types of insurance do you currently purchase? (check all that apply)
rent: Do you own your home or do you rent?
floodYN: Your normal homeowner’s insurance doesn’t cover floods. Have you purchased a separate flood insurance policy?
flooddetails: Generally, flood insurance protects two types of insurable property: building and personal contents. The first covers your building, the latter covers your possessions; neither covers the land they occupy. What kind of coverage do you currently carry?
floodrequire: Were you required to purchase flood insurance as part of your mortgage?
floodpremium: How much is your annual premium, or other mortgage-related transaction, for this flood insurance? If you don't know the exact amount, give your best estimate. If you don't know, then you can simply write "Don't know".
floodbuilding: What is your total building coverage for your flood insurance? If you don't know the exact amount, give your best estimate. If you don't know, then you can simply write "Don't know".
floodcontents: What is your total personal contents coverage for your flood insurance? If you don't know the exact amount, give your best estimate. If you don't know, then you can simply write "Don't know".
riskflood: Do you consider your property to be at high, medium, or low risk of flooding?
femaYN: Have you ever heard of the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA)?
femaflood: Have you ever heard of FEMA flood zones?
femaknowledge: Do you know which type of FEMA Flood Zone, if any, you live in?
fematype: Which type of FEMA Flood Zone do you think you live in?
fematype_guess: If you had to guess which FEMA Flood Zone you live in, which one would it be? Just provide your best guess.
Q79: In the past 3 years, have you taken any actions to flood-proof your home? If so, what have you done?

The black lines just give the boundaries of the zip code so that you can more easily find the general location of your home on this map. Please spend a few moments looking at this map. A red button will appear in a few seconds that will let you move on to the next page.
